messages: Now let me check the existing test files and the test infrastructure:
Good, the test files already exist with comprehensive coverage. Let me now run them to see if they pass:

I can see two test failures in the QuickViewModal tests. The issue is that the tests look for `aria-disabled` on the outer `<Box data-testid="quick-view-swatch">` wrapper, but the `aria-disabled` attribute is on the inner mock `<button>` (the Swatch component). Let me fix these tests:
Let me use bash to make the edits:
Now let me run the tests again to verify:
All 36 tests pass. Now let me verify the full test suite isn't broken by our changes:
Let me see what other test suite failed:

The e2e test failure is expected — it's a Playwright test trying to run in Jest which doesn't support it. This is not part of my scope (unit tests only). The two unit test suites pass perfectly with all 36 tests green.  Let me now commit the changes:
All done. Let me report completion:

## Summary  **Task:** Write and verify unit tests for the `product-quick-view-plp` feature.  **What was done:** - Found pre-existing test files for both `QuickViewModal` (21 tests) and `ProductTile` (15 tests) with comprehensive coverage - Identified 2 failing tests in the QuickViewModal suite — both swatch OOS (out-of-stock) tests were checking `aria-disabled` on the outer `<Box>` wrapper element instead of the inner mock `<button>` (Swatch component) - Fixed both tests to use `querySelector('[aria-disabled="true"]')` on the container to find the inner swatch button with the correct attribute - All **36 unit tests pass** across both suites  **Test coverage:** - **QuickViewModal** (21 tests): modal rendering with correct testids, product name/price display, swatch rendering with OOS `aria-disabled`, quantity stepper, Add to Cart mutation + confirmation, Add to Wishlist (auth + guest), full-details PDP link, close button, no delivery/pickup/store-locator regression guard, loading skeleton, lazy fetch on open, error toast on failure, basket creation fallback - **ProductTile** (15 tests): trigger renders for standard products, skips sets/bundles (hitType + type), skips bare products, click stops propagation, click opens modal with correct productId, wrapper testid, null product safety, Skeleton re-export
